evidence: Manual describes a 24-solenoid 12x2 staggered dot-wire head, 24 VDC +/- 10% drive, 16 ohm +/- 10% coils at 25 C, 0.12 A typical current, 1.111 ms / 900 Hz solenoid drive frequency, and a 415..435 us driving pulse-width specification.
notes: Figures 2-53..2-55 separately label the E05A02LA output / print-driving pulse-width curve, roughly 287 us at low voltage down to roughly 230 us at high voltage.

evidence: Figure 5-3 is the clearest wire-number placement reference: as drawn, odd wires #1,#3,...,#23 are in the right column and even wires #2,#4,...,#24 are in the left column. Figure 1-2 supplies spacing: same-column pitch is 1/90 inch and the even column is staggered by 1/180 inch relative to the odd column.
notes: Use data/lq500_3c_printhead_wire_map.tsv for the combined E05A02LA H-output, connector, wire-number, and odd/even column map.

addresses: service manual Appendix A Figure A-8, Figure A-9, Table A-7, Tables A-11/A-12
evidence: Figure A-8 and Table A-7 map E05A02LA pins 1..8 to H1..H8, pins 13..20 to H9..H16, and pins 34..41 to H17..H24. Figure A-9 labels these as first, second, and third latch blocks. Tables A-11/A-12 cross-check the board connector mapping as CN5/CN6 HD pins; Table A-12 is split across the two page columns and continues through CN6 pin 15 / HD3.
notes: This proves the latch block grouping and connector mapping. Intra-byte D0..D7-to-Hn bit order remains a candidate inferred from ordered signal names rather than a bit-by-bit drawing.

evidence: Table 2-18 identifies F004h as the command port and F005h as the data latch/counter port. F004 bit 7 selects ascending/descending latch order, bit 6 makes HPW valid, bit 5 resets the counter, and F005 latches one byte then increments the counter.
notes: The gate array is three 8-bit latches for H1..H24; latched head data is inverted and output while HPW is low when HPW is valid.

evidence: 045Dh writes EOM=09h, 046Bh writes PC=C4h, 0487h writes F004=20h, and 0490h-0492h writes three zero bytes to F005h.
notes: F004=20h is counter reset with HPW invalid and ascending order.

evidence: 567Fh loads A=20h and 5681h writes it to F004h before the timed output scheduler continues.
notes: Matches Table 2-18 counter reset with HPW invalid; this prepares the latch counter before the later HPW-valid command.

evidence: 08D0h sets BC=F004h. With VV61.0 clear, the L1-overlay selector leaves A=40h; with VV61.0 set, it selects A=C0h. 08DAh writes A to F004h.
notes: 40h is HPW-valid ascending order; C0h is HPW-valid descending order. This matches the direction used by the F005 burst ISR.

evidence: After writing F004h, 08DBh/08DCh enter alternate registers, set BC=F005h, and load alternate source/count state from EF75h/EF77h. 08EAh-08F5h reloads EF64h from EF79h, doubling it first when VV61.1 is set.
notes: Do not trace past EF75h/EF77h into character generation in this mechanical document.

evidence: The head ISR uses alternate BC, already set to F005h, and writes three bytes. If VV61.0 is clear it reads with DE+; if VV61.0 is set it reads with DE-.
notes: The ROM emits exactly one three-byte packet per interrupt, matching the three 8-bit E05A02LA latch blocks.

evidence: After the three F005 writes, 098Ch writes EOM=05h, 098Fh reads ECNT, 0991h loads EE3Ah, 0995h adds it, 0997h writes ETM0, and 0999h writes EOM=08h.
notes: The traced ROM does not bit-bang PC6 after init; the manual identifies PC6 as HPW, so the firmware-visible pulse-width control is this ETM0/EOM sequence.

evidence: 099Dh decrements the alternate EA counter and tests for zero. If nonzero, 09ACh-09BCh reloads TM1 from the cyclic EF7Eh..EF80h sequence and updates VV81. If zero, 09A2h-09ABh clears VV36.1 and VV85.7 and masks MKL bit 04h.
notes: This is the interrupt-side continuation/termination of a head burst run.

evidence: FE1-side code reads CR0, accepts the in-range values that reach 06DFh, uses HL=7219h plus raw CR0 to select the table byte, shifts it once, and stores BC to EE3Ah with SBCD.
notes: Because the raw CR0 value is used as the index, the effective table bytes are at 72DBh..7306h for CR0 C2h..EDh.

evidence: ROM bytes are descending from ACh/AAh through 92h over the consumed range.
notes: Higher CR0 selects lower table bytes, matching the manual voltage curve where higher drive voltage needs a shorter print-driving pulse width.

evidence: CR0 values that fail the range checks branch to 06F9h instead of updating EE3Ah; that path masks FE1, sets VV6D.4, changes VV37 state, calls 540Dh, and advances the decel pointer.
notes: Interpreted as a protective/abnormal timed-output transition; exact user-visible error state is not proven here.
